Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Up To 16 Hours: British Airways' 10 Longest Nonstop Flights From London [2026]

British Airways is the world's fifth-largest long-haul operator by number of flights. Only United Airlines, Emirates, Qatar Airways, and Delta Air Lines have more long-haul services. Between now and the end of October, BA has an average of 95 daily long-haul departures from London, up from 91...

British Airways' expansion to ultra-long-haul routes reflects growing demand for direct transatlantic and Asia-Pacific services, positioning the carrier competitively against US and Middle Eastern carriers in premium long-distance markets.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

ITA Airways officially joins Star Alliance

Italian airline ITA Airways has marked its official accession to Star Alliance, which will become effective on April…...

ITA Airways' Star Alliance entry expands the alliance's European network and provides Italian travelers seamless connectivity across 26 member carriers' global routes.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Antaris™ Raises $28 Million Series A to Accelerate AI-Driven Space Missions

Los Altos, California — Antaris™, creator of the AI-powered Antaris Intelligence™ platform that simplifies satellite design, simulation, manufacturing, and operations for ISR and communications satellite missions, today announced the first close […]...

AI-powered satellite design automation could significantly reduce development cycles and costs for operators managing ISR and communications fleets.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 31, 2026

More A-10s Are Headed to the Middle East

The deployment of additional A-10Cs to the Middle East, the first of which arrived at RAF Lakenheath, demonstrates the continued reliance on the close air support aircraft for operations in Iran. The U.S. Air Force A-10C Thunderbolt II jets have been actively supporting Operation Epic Fury in Iran...

The A-10's persistent deployment to counter Iranian threats signals the Air Force's confidence in close air support capabilities despite ongoing modernization debates.

See The Aviationist →

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally document their Safety Management System processes to FAA standards, with non-compliance risking enforcement action and operational restrictions.

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

Trump International? One Final Hurdle Left For Palm Beach Airport's Renaming

The name change hasn't been finalized yet, as it still requires FAA approval.

FAA approval remains the final regulatory requirement before the Palm Beach airport can officially adopt its new name, adding a procedural delay to the rebranding timeline.

PR-DC Introduces the First Military-Certified Multicopter Capable of Launching Three Aircraft Rockets

Following successful internal testing, PR-DC announces the introduction of what it describes as the world’s...

Military certification of air-launch-capable drones enables distributed munitions delivery from smaller platforms, reducing fixed-base infrastructure dependency.

DroneShield, a global leader in counter-drone technology solutions, along with Origin Robotics, a defence technology...

DroneShield's partnership with Origin Robotics signals the counter-drone market's shift toward integrated kinetic solutions beyond detection and jamming.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS frameworks shift aviation safety from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen.

ideaForge's partnership with IIT Bombay creates a pipeline to identify deep-tech founders who could accelerate drone and autonomous systems innovation in India's aerospace ecosystem.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

IndiGo names ex-British Airways boss and current IATA head Willie Walsh as CEO

Low-cost-carrier IndiGo has named the former British Airways boss and current director general of International Air Transport Association…...

Walsh's appointment signals IndiGo's strategic shift toward international expansion and industry advocacy as it matures beyond its domestic low-cost model.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 12 Airlines That Still Operate 4-Engine Passenger Jets

Only three families of passenger quadjets remain in service around the world with a contracting number of commercial airlines.

The dwindling quadjet fleet reflects how twin-engine efficiency and longer-range capability have made four-engine aircraft economically unviable for most carriers.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure from RNO requires heightened pilot attention due to procedure complexity that differs meaningfully from standard departures at this high-altitude airport.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

International Mar 31, 2026

Poland rules out Patriot redeployment after US request reported

The United States has informally asked Poland to consider redeploying one of its two Patriot air defense batteries…...

Poland's refusal to redeploy Patriot systems highlights constraints on NATO air defense coordination during ongoing regional security challenges.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

China targets 140 launches in 2026 amid commercial space surge

China plans to conduct around 140 orbital launches this year, according to a commercial launch executive, marking a sharp acceleration in the country’s launch cadence.

China's 140-launch target for 2026 represents a near-tripling of recent capacity, signaling intensified competition for satellite constellation deployment and launch services globally.

See SpaceNews →

International Mar 31, 2026

Iran war forces IATA to cut global seat capacity growth projections

The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has said that global capacity growth eased to 3.3% year on year…...

Geopolitical instability is now a quantifiable factor in demand forecasting, with IATA reducing capacity projections as airlines preemptively adjust fleet deployment strategies amid Middle East tensions.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

KSQL Noise Abatement Departure

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures must navigate dense Bay Area terrain and SFO's Class B airspace, making departure routing exceptionally constrained for operators.

Massive 25-Hour Flights: Qantas' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Huge changes are coming to Qantas' longest services. See them all here!

Qantas' ultra-long routes demand aircraft with extended range capabilities and crew rest provisions, reshaping pilot scheduling and aircraft maintenance planning for airlines pursuing similar strategies.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

AI drones are now catching aerial threats like flying cops

XTEND is doubling down on one of the biggest challenges in modern warfare: how to stop hostile drones, without making things worse in the process. The AI-driven robotics company, backed by Eric Trump, has announced a new partnership with ParaZero Technologies as part of its broader $1.5 billion...

Counter-drone AI systems now require safety-parachute integration to prevent collateral damage when neutralizing aerial threats.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Two EA-37Bs Arrive at RAF Mildenhall on Possible First Operational Deployment

Two EA-37B Compass Call aircraft stopped at RAF Mildenhall for fuel during what may be the type’s first operational deployment abroad, reports suggesting Turkey as their destination. Two U.S. Air Force EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft have arrived at RAF Mildenhall, UK, on Mar. 31,...

The EA-37B Compass Call's potential first operational deployment signals escalating U.S. electronic warfare capabilities in geopolitically sensitive regions.

See The Aviationist →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across the aviation system can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impact as traffic grows, requiring integrated improvements rather than siloed technological fixes.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Europe’s space sector faces power shift as funding grows

New report from Aerospace Corp. highlights rising EU funding and defense focus...

European aerospace competitiveness is increasingly dependent on defense spending rather than commercial innovation, signaling a strategic realignment in space sector development.

See SpaceNews →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

DroneShield opens Amsterdam HQ as counter-drone demand grows

Drone threats aren’t slowing down in Europe, and neither is DroneShield. The Australia-based counter-drone and electronic warfare specialist has officially opened its new European headquarters in Amsterdam, signaling a deeper, more permanent push into one of its fastest-growing markets. more…...

DroneShield's European expansion reflects accelerating regulatory and security pressure on airspace management as unauthorized drone operations become a critical infrastructure concern across the continent.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Embraer KC-390 Millennium showcases cold weather operations in Sweden

Embraer’s KC-390 Millennium demonstrator has completed short takeoff and landing operations at Sweden’s Vidsel Test Range as part of the…...

KC-390's Arctic testing validates operational capability in extreme climates, expanding addressable markets beyond traditional equatorial/tropical deployments for military transport operators.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Sphere ditches contractors, speeds up HubT drone deployments

Sphere is tightening its grip on autonomous drone deployments with a set of practical upgrades to its HubT platform, but the biggest shift is how the system is now built. more…...

Sphere's vertical integration of drone deployment operations reduces third-party dependencies while improving speed-to-market for autonomous systems.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Researchers say SAF made from solar energy could be scaled to industrial levels

The University of Sheffield has developed a new way of making sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using solar energy…...

Solar-derived SAF production could fundamentally reduce aviation's carbon footprint if manufacturing scalability challenges are successfully overcome in the next 5-10 years.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

UK drone rules just got way easier to understand

Grey Arrows Drone Club just did something UK drone pilots have quietly needed for years. And if you’ve ever second-guessed where you’re allowed to take off, this could be a game-changer. more…...

Simplified guidance on UK drone takeoff regulations could reduce operational confusion and potential compliance violations among recreational and commercial pilots.

Airline dependency on a single aircraft type concentrates operational risk around Boeing's 737 supply chain and certification challenges.

Rheinmetall and Boeing Australia formalize MQ-28 Ghost Bat bid

Germany’s interest in acquiring the MQ-28 Ghost Bat has moved from diplomatic signals to a formal industrial partnership in a…...

Germany's formal partnership with Boeing on the MQ-28 signals European defense procurement is increasingly shifting toward autonomous unmanned systems as piloted alternatives face rising costs and pilot availability constraints.

Bicopters' theoretical efficiency advantage translates to practical endurance gains, enabling longer mission durations than traditional quadcopters in size-constrained applications.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

2026 might be the most affordable year to buy a DJI drone

DJI drones that once felt like serious investments are now showing up at prices that would’ve seemed unrealistic even a year ago. Thanks to the Amazon Big Spring Sale, models across the lineup are seeing steep discounts, opening the door at just $149 and making it very easy to imagine how different...

Consumer drone pricing compression driven by retail competition is expanding market accessibility beyond professional and enthusiast segments into mainstream consumer adoption.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Drone survey without GCPs? Wingtra claims sub-3 cm accuracy

Wingtra is stepping into 2026 with a clear message for the surveying world: the future of mapping might not need ground control points at all. The Switzerland-based drone company recently launched the SURVEY61 payload, designed to work with the WingtraRAY drone. And the pitch is hard to ignore —...

Wingtra's new SURVEY61 payload eliminates the need for ground control points while maintaining sub-3cm accuracy, potentially reducing surveying mission costs and timeline complexity.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Sikorsky Collaborates with Robinson Helicopter Company to Integrate MATRIX™ Autonomy into Robinson R66 TURBINETRUCK

Sikorsky, a Lockheed Martin company (NYSE:LMT), and Robinson Helicopter Company (RHC) unveiled the R66 TURBINETRUCK, a game-changing...

Integrating Sikorsky's autonomous technology into Robinson's established R66 platform accelerates commercialization of autonomous rotorcraft without requiring manufacturers to develop proprietary flight control systems from scratch.

Artemis 2 countdown underway

The two-day countdown for the Artemis 2 mission around the moon started March 30 with NASA officials reporting no major issues for the launch.

NASA's Artemis 2 readiness demonstrates critical progress toward sustainable lunar exploration infrastructure that will enable commercial space ventures beyond Earth orbit.

Inside The Boeing 747's Upper Deck Layout

From a secluded cocktail bar to a high-density seating zone, the evolution of the 747's upper deck reflects the changing priorities of aviation.

The 747's upper deck evolved from luxury amenities to revenue-maximizing seating, illustrating how fleet economics have fundamentally shifted aircraft interior design priorities.
